microarchitectural_signatures.rationale: WAL append, sync, and checkpoint service time are measured directly on the commit-finalize path, so this cell is a genuine durability-pressure lane rather than a generic executor slowdown.

microarchitectural_signatures.rationale: Measured page-lock wait time is the clearest MVCC coordination lane, and cache-to-cache or remote-access captures determine whether we can say more than an explicit mixed contention story.

microarchitectural_signatures.rationale: BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK boundary time is visible, and the split WAL lock-wait plus wake evidence distinguishes coordination topology from durable append/sync service without inventing a single cause.
microarchitectural_signatures.implication: A/E target: transaction-boundary coordination is still a first-class wall-time tax and should be pushed toward narrower residual serialized regions.

microarchitectural_signatures.rationale: Copy-heavy row materialization is the clearest cache/memory lane in the hot path, and the capture pack includes cache-oriented evidence, so LLC/TLB pressure is the most defensible signature pair here.
microarchitectural_signatures.implication: J target: allocator and copy work is large enough to justify scratch-space reuse, row-value ownership reduction, and reusable buffers.

causal_classification.rationale: `retries` leads the concrete bucket ranking by 3790 score bps over `synchronization` using classified hot-path time derived from the existing wall-time, waste-ledger, and cost-component artifacts.

causal_buckets.rationale: Retries bucket isolates configured backoff sleep and retry counters so passive waiting is not conflated with queueing or synchronization work.
causal_buckets.implication: Retry-dominant rows are losing wall time to configured backoff, which is distinct from in-attempt queueing and should stay explicit.

evidence.rationale: retry bucket maps to explicit configured backoff sleep rather than in-attempt wait time
runtime phase timing already exposes aggregate retry-backoff sleep directly
top-level engine retries keep the wall-time lane tied to observed retry frequency
structured retry taxonomy refines the retry bucket without collapsing it into generic queueing
abort counts remain adjacent evidence for retry-heavy rows that fail to converge cleanly

causal_buckets.rationale: Synchronization bucket intentionally aggregates executor boundary coordination, MVCC page-lock wait, and commit-surface maintenance because those are the explicit coordination counters the code exposes today.
causal_buckets.implication: Synchronization-dominant rows are paying boundary or MVCC coordination cost; keep publish-plane and page-topology work ahead of generic copy tuning.

evidence.rationale: boundary coordination already has its own explicit wall-time component
page-lock handoff wait is the strongest direct MVCC coordination counter the profile exposes today
page-one tracking plus pending-surface maintenance are already split out as a separate coordination lane
VFS lock activity is emitted in the hot-path profile and helps explain synchronization-heavy rows without inventing a new counter

causal_buckets.rationale: Queueing bucket uses the explicit BUSY-attempt wall-time lane and keeps it separate from retry-backoff sleep.
causal_buckets.implication: Queueing-dominant rows are spending time inside failed BUSY attempts; steer fixes toward contention topology rather than allocator cleanup.

evidence.rationale: queueing is the wall-time lane for time spent inside BUSY attempts that later retried
runtime phase timing captures the raw busy-attempt wall time directly
the spillover ledger keeps busy-attempt queueing visible alongside the separate retry-backoff lane

causal_buckets.rationale: Service bucket is anchored to executor body time after allocator-copy work is carved out, then cross-checked against parser/decode/materialization artifacts already emitted in the actionable ranking.
causal_buckets.implication: Service-dominant rows should be explained with parser/decode/materialization evidence before blaming contention or durability.

evidence.rationale: service starts from executor body time after the explicit allocator-copy carve-out

causal_buckets.rationale: Allocation bucket starts with explicit allocator-copy wall time and is reinforced by emitted heap-byte and page-normalization artifacts.

causal_buckets.rationale: I/O bucket maps directly onto the durability wall-time lane and the VFS/WAL byte, frame, and sync counters already present in the raw profile artifact.

WAL frames written quantify commit-path write volume without needing a new artifact
